source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design and deliver the primary structural assembly of a CanSat complying with the national aerospace student benchmark 2025 Model Uydu Yarışması şartnamesi, modelled as a multi-body assembly rather than a single monolithic part. The complete CanSat including all payloads, batteries, electronics, and recovery hardware must fit within a cylindrical envelope of outer diameter 66 mm (-0/+1 mm) and total height 115 mm (-0/+2 mm), with total integrated wet mass between 300 g and 350 g. The original rulebook prompt names four distinct part families that together form the structure: a thin-wall cylindrical outer skin, a conical nose cap, a base plate with parachute eyelet, and three internal PCB-stack rails spaced 120 degrees around the inner wall. In this multi-part rendering the structure is decomposed into six named bodies: (A) one cylindrical outer shell (skin), (B) one conical nose cap, (C) one base plate with parachute eyelet, (D) three PCB-stack rails (count_min = 3, one per 120-degree station), and two supporting interface families, (E) the joining hardware family that fixes nose and base to the shell (small countersunk screws or heat-stake pins; team-selected) and (F) the bonded or mechanical interface family between each rail and the inner shell wall. The CanSat is launched inside a carrier rocket to an altitude of approximately 700 m AGL, ejected, and descends under a single parachute with a nominal descent rate between 6 m/s and 8 m/s. Material is 3D-printed PA12-CF (carbon-fiber reinforced nylon, SLS) with Young's modulus 5.8 GPa, tensile strength 75 MPa, density 1060 kg/m^3. Load cases preserved from the monolithic sample: (LC1) 20 g quasi-static axial acceleration applied to the full CanSat wet mass of 350 g; (LC2) 15 g lateral acceleration simultaneously along the worst-case transverse direction; (LC3) parachute opening shock of 40 N applied at the base-plate eyelet in the axial direction while the CanSat hangs from the shock cord; (LC4) ground-impact drop of 350 g at 8 m/s onto a rigid flat surface, sized as an equivalent static 10 g deceleration on the base. Pass/fail criteria are unchanged: (R1) max von Mises stress in the primary structure below 37.5 MPa under all load cases (75/2.0 material FoS); (R2) max deflection of any PCB stack rail relative to the outer skin below 1.5 mm under LC1; (R3) no structural-element first natural frequency below 80 Hz; (R4) total structural dry mass (shell + nose + base + rails, excluding PCB/battery/parachute) not to exceed 120 g; (R5) the shell outer surface must not protrude past the 66 mm envelope under any load case. Because this sidecar treats the structure as an assembly, two additional assembly-level observables are reported: (A1) the maximum relative deflection between the nose cap and the shell at the bonded/joined interface IF1 under LC1 and LC3, and (A2) the maximum connection demand/capacity ratio at IF1 and IF2 (nose-to-shell and base-to-shell interfaces) and at the rail-to-shell interfaces IF3..IF5, each evaluated against the selected joint's shear / tensile capacity. These are per-connection reports and are not gating pass/fail criteria; pass/fail is still driven by R1..R5 evaluated on the assembled model. Vibration qualification is satisfied by meeting R3 plus the LC1/LC2 quasi-static envelope; no random-vibration PSD is imposed at this level. Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 multi-body solid model with one body per named part family plus one body per rail instance, SLS process note (build orientation and support plan per body), FEA report with stress + deflection for each load case over the fully assembled model, per-part max stress reported but not gating, assembly deflection at IF1, connection DCR table, and modal report listing the first 10 eigenfrequencies of the assembled CanSat.
short_prompt:
Design and deliver the primary structural assembly of a CanSat complying with the national aerospace student benchmark 2025 Model Uydu Yarışması şartnamesi, modelled as a multi-body assembly.

Envelope: cylindrical, outer diameter 66 mm (-0/+1 mm), total height 115 mm (-0/+2 mm). Integrated wet mass 300 g to 350 g. CanSat launched in carrier rocket to approximately 700 m AGL, descends under single parachute at 6 m/s to 8 m/s.

Material: 3D-printed PA12-CF (carbon-fiber reinforced nylon, SLS), tensile 75 MPa, density 1060 kg/m^3.

Load cases: (LC1) 20 g quasi-static axial applied to 350 g wet mass; (LC2) 15 g lateral simultaneously along worst-case transverse; (LC3) parachute opening shock 40 N axial at base-plate eyelet; (LC4) ground-impact drop 350 g at 8 m/s, sized as equivalent static 10 g deceleration on base.

Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises < 37.5 MPa across all LCs (75/2.0 FoS); (R2) PCB-rail relative deflection to skin < 1.5 mm under LC1; (R3) first natural frequency >= 80 Hz; (R4) structural dry mass (shell + nose + base + rails) <= 120 g; (R5) shell outer surface stays within 66 mm envelope under any LC.

prompt.title: national aerospace student benchmark 2025 Model Uydu Primary CanSat Structure (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: 350 g CanSat primary shell + nose + base + 3 PCB-stack rails (multi-body assembly) inside 66 x 115 mm envelope, 20 g axial + 15 g lateral launch qual, parachute descent 6-8 m/s; assembled-only FEA pass/fail, per-part stresses reported.

parts.name: outer_cylindrical_shell
parts.role: primary load path carrying axial launch and lateral loads; defines outer envelope

parts.name: conical_nose_cap
parts.role: aerodynamic nose closure at top of CanSat; bonded or mechanically fastened to shell

parts.name: base_plate_with_parachute_eyelet
parts.role: reacts parachute opening shock and ground-impact deceleration; houses M3/M4 eyelet insert

parts.name: internal_pcb_stack_rail
parts.role: PCB-stack support rail, one per 120-degree station, bonded or heat-staked to inner shell wall
parts.count_note: Exactly 3 rails per original sample (three internal PCB-stack rails spaced 120 degrees).

parts.name: shell_end_fastener
parts.role: fastens nose cap and base plate to shell (small screws or heat-stake pins)
parts.count_note: At least 3 per end (nose/base), typical for balanced load distribution.

geometric_constraints.nominal_size: M2 to M3 or heat-stake pin diameter 2-3 mm

parts.material_hint: stainless or integrated polymer pin; team-selected

parts.name: rail_to_shell_bond
parts.role: bonded or mechanically joined interface between each rail and inner shell wall
parts.material_hint: structural adhesive or polymer weld; team-selected

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: cylinder outer_diameter 66 mm (-0/+1) x height 115 mm (-0/+2)
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: z-axis along launch vehicle axis (axial); x-y in base plane; origin at base-plate center

load_cases.name: axial_launch_20g
load_cases.application: axial on full 350 g wet mass

load_cases.name: lateral_launch_15g
load_cases.application: transverse on full 350 g wet mass

load_cases.name: ground_impact
load_cases.application: base equivalent static for 8 m/s drop

boundary_conditions.rigid_body_suppression: pinned at CanSat centerline during quasi-static launch load application; base eyelet pinned during LC3
boundary_conditions.connections: IF1 nose-to-shell (joint type team-selected), IF2 base-to-shell, IF3..IF5 each PCB rail to inner shell wall; connection stiffness = equivalent linear spring for each E/F instance (screw shank axial + shear or adhesive bond line).

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: TET10 solid for shell, nose, base, rails; tied/coupled connectors at IF1..IF5 with equivalent stiffness

interfaces.load_path: axial tension/compression + small moment from nose to shell under launch
axial tension under parachute shock, axial compression under ground impact
shear along bond line under LC1/LC2 transmitted from PCB stack mass to shell
small axial/tangential reaction where end fasteners close nose and base to shell

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: Original sample names four distinct part families (shell, nose, base, 3 rails) that cannot share a single printed body without sacrificing build orientation and rail alignment.
notes.speculation_caveats: Fastener family E and bond family F are speculative; team-selected count and type. IF1..IF2 may be bonded, screwed, or snap-fit.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
